steps.title: Isolate the absolute value expression
steps.content:
Start with $-2|3x + 10| + 12 = -8$. Subtract 12 from both sides:
$$-2|3x + 10| = -20$$
Divide both sides by $-2$:
$$|3x + 10| = 10$$

steps.title: Apply the absolute value definition to find both solutions
steps.content:
The equation $|3x + 10| = 10$ means the expression inside is either $10$ or $-10$.

Case 1: When $3x + 10 = 10$:
$$3x = 0$$
$$x = 0$$
Case 2: When $3x + 10 = -10$:
$$3x = -20$$
$$x = -\frac{20}{3}$$
The solutions are $x = 0$ and $x = -\frac{20}{3}$. That confirms C is correct.
